rubric.target_patient: Women or men with HR-positive, HER2-negative, locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer whose tumor harbors a PIK3CA mutation, who progressed on/shortly after adjuvant endocrine therapy (AI or tamoxifen) and have received NO prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease — i.e., a first-line metastatic HR+/HER2- population enriched for a specific genomic biomarker, requiring central and/or local PIK3CA testing before enrollment.
rubric.site_burden: Based on the posted Schedule of Assessments heading (weak evidence, heading-only confidence), the protocol requires: baseline collection of both blood and tumor tissue for central PIK3CA testing (with liquid biopsy fallback via FoundationOne Liquid CDx if local results unavailable), tumor imaging every 8 weeks for 2 years then every 12 weeks, PK blood sampling at multiple timepoints, close AE monitoring including a distinct hyperglycemia follow-up pathway (inavolisib is a PI3K inhibitor with known hyperglycemia risk), multiple patient-reported outcome instruments (QLQ-C30, QLQ-BR23, BPI-SF, PRO-CTCAE, EQ-5D-5L) administered per schedule, long-term survival follow-up approximately every 3 months post-discontinuation, and optional mobile-nursing infrastructure at approved sites. This implies a site needs biomarker testing/specimen logistics, an established infusion/oral-oncolytic administration capability, PRO data capture systems, and experience managing metabolic AEs (hyperglycemia) associated with PI3K inhibitors — but the exact visit-by-visit grid was not available in the provided material.

criteria.requirement: The investigator must be a medical oncologist (or hematology-oncology specialist) with a breast cancer-focused practice, not a general oncologist with incidental breast cases.
criteria.satisfies_if: NPPES taxonomy lists Hematology/Oncology or Medical Oncology, AND physician has a documented history of treating breast cancer patients (e.g., prior breast cancer trial roles or publications).
criteria.derived_from: Conditions: Breast Cancer; population is HR+/HER2- locally advanced/metastatic breast cancer requiring specialist management

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have familiarity with PIK3CA-mutant breast cancer biology and biomarker-driven treatment selection, given this is a molecularly-selected trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician has authored/co-authored at least one publication on PIK3CA mutation, PI3K pathway inhibitors, or biomarker-selected HR+/HER2- breast cancer therapy, OR has prior investigator role on a PI3K/AKT/mTOR-inhibitor breast cancer trial.
criteria.derived_from: Study evaluates inavolisib (PI3K inhibitor) specifically in PIK3CA-mutant HR+/HER2- breast cancer; exclusion of prior PI3K/AKT/mTOR inhibitor use signals disease area specialization needed

criteria.requirement: The practice must regularly see treatment-naive (first-line metastatic) HR+/HER2- breast cancer patients progressing on/after adjuvant endocrine therapy, since prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease is exclusionary.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician/practice can attest to managing a first-line metastatic HR+/HER2- breast cancer caseload sufficient to identify multiple eligible patients per year (self-reported caseload estimate), or Medicare claims show a floor volume of breast cancer E&M/chemotherapy administration codes consistent with a dedicated breast oncology practice.
criteria.derived_from: Inclusion: no prior systemic therapy for metastatic disease; progression during/within 12 months of adjuvant endocrine therapy

criteria.requirement: The site must have practical access to PIK3CA mutation testing (local CLIA-certified lab or ability to route samples to central/liquid biopsy testing) as part of routine or trial-driven workup.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to an established workflow for tumor genomic testing (local NGS panel or central lab submission) as part of standard practice or via trial infrastructure.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: patients without local PIK3CA results must submit blood/tumor samples for central F1LCDx testing; all patients must provide blood and tumor tissue

criteria.requirement: Site must have infrastructure for chronic oral oncolytic dispensing/adherence tracking (palbociclib, inavolisib/placebo) plus recurring intramuscular fulvestrant administration in 28-day cycles.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to on-site or affiliated capability for IM drug administration and oral oncolytic dispensing/monitoring on a recurring cycle basis (e.g., existing CDK4/6 inhibitor + fulvestrant regimen experience).
criteria.derived_from: Interventions: oral inavolisib/placebo Days 1-28, oral palbociclib Days 1-21, IM fulvestrant every 4 weeks, in 28-day cycles

criteria.requirement: Site should have capability to monitor and manage treatment-emergent hyperglycemia, including a defined post-treatment hyperglycemia follow-up pathway, given inavolisib's known metabolic toxicity.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to protocols or endocrinology referral pathways for managing anti-hyperglycemic therapy and monitoring beyond standard oncology AE management.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: post-30-day hyperglycemia follow-up for patients on anti-hyperglycemic agents or with ongoing hyperglycemia events

criteria.requirement: Site must be able to administer and capture multiple patient-reported outcome instruments (QLQ-C30, QLQ-BR23, BPI-SF, PRO-CTCAE, EQ-5D-5L) per the study schedule.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to existing PRO data collection infrastructure (electronic or paper) used in prior industry trials.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: multiple PRO instruments (QLQ-C30, QLQ-BR23, BPI-SF, PRO-CTCAE, EQ-5D-5L) specified in schedule of activities

criteria.requirement: Site must sustain long-term survival follow-up (approx. every 3 months post-discontinuation, up to 3.7 years total study duration) via phone or clinic visits.
criteria.satisfies_if: Site attests to established procedures for long-duration survival follow-up contact and retention in prior long-term oncology trials.
criteria.derived_from: Operational burden: survival/subsequent anti-cancer therapy follow-up approximately every 3 months until death/withdrawal; primary outcome timeframe up to 3.7 years

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have prior experience as an investigator on industry-sponsored oncology trials, given this is a large, complex Roche Phase II/III registration-track study with 135 global sites.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician is listed as principal investigator or sub-investigator on >=1 completed or ongoing industry-sponsored breast cancer clinical trial in clinicaltrials.gov.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: Hoffmann-La Roche (INDUSTRY); Phase2/Phase3, 135 sites globally, complex multi-drug regimen

criteria.requirement: Investigator should have a demonstrable relationship with breast oncology drug sponsors (e.g., Roche/Genentech or related CDK4/6i or PI3K-pathway drug makers), suggesting familiarity with sponsor trial conduct expectations.
criteria.satisfies_if: Open Payments records show research payments from Roche/Genentech or Pfizer (palbociclib manufacturer) within the last 5 years.
criteria.derived_from: Sponsor: F. Hoffmann-La Roche Ltd; combination includes palbociclib (Pfizer) and fulvestrant

criteria.requirement: Site should be part of an established research network or academic cancer center with GCP-trained staff capable of running a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled multi-drug trial.
criteria.satisfies_if: Physician's practice matches or is affiliated with one of the listed site institutions (e.g., Sarah Cannon/Tennessee Oncology, Texas Oncology, academic medical center) or physician self-reports GCP certification and dedicated research staff.
criteria.derived_from: Site footprint sample: Sarah Cannon Research Institute/Tennessee Oncology, Texas Oncology, MSKCC, MGH, academic and large community oncology networks

excluded_screening: ECOG Performance Status 0-1 — assessed per patient at screening, not a site characteristic
Life expectancy > 6 months — clinical judgment per patient, irrelevant to site qualification
Adequate hematologic/organ function within 14 days prior to treatment — per-patient lab threshold at enrollment
Anti-cancer therapy within 2 weeks before study entry — per-patient washout timing
Investigational drug(s) within 4 weeks before randomization — per-patient washout timing
Pregnancy/lactation/contraception intent — per-patient eligibility, not physician-fit
Major surgery or significant trauma within 28 days of Day 1 — per-patient timing exclusion
Menopausal status definitions and LHRH agonist requirements for pre/perimenopausal women or men — per-patient eligibility mechanics, not a practice-level capability
Histologic confirmation of ER/PR positivity and HER2-negativity by ASCO/CAP criteria on most recent biopsy — per-patient diagnostic confirmation, not investigator fit
